title: Exact Finite Euler Method Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite Euler bad fixed-step, bad terminal-error, and bad max-error QF_LRA/Farkas regression seeds
solver_reuse.pressure: Malformed finite Euler rows where exact replay forces next_state = state + (1/2)*derivative = 1/2 but a row claims next_state = 3/4, computes terminal error 3/4 but a row claims terminal error 1/2, and computes max error 3/4 but a row claims max_error <= 1/2.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional finite-error or invariant rows only when they add distinct exact-linear solver pressure; keep continuous-time existence, uniqueness, stability, stiffness, convergence-rate, and PDE claims in Lean-horizon or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode fixed Euler-step and finite-error checks as deterministic QF_LRA transition obligations.
Replay SAT witnesses through Axeyum model evaluation rather than pack-local Python only.
Route false fixed-step transition, finite terminal-error, and finite error-bound rows through checked QF_LRA/Farkas certificates once derivatives and error tables are replayed exactly.
Keep continuous-time existence, uniqueness, stability, stiffness, convergence-rate, and PDE claims under Lean horizon or numerical-honesty metadata until formal routes exist.
